_comment: Release-tag protection RULESET for this repository. A `v*` tag triggers a
production deploy (build-image.yml dispatches to the deploy pipeline), so
creating one MUST be restricted to maintainers. Branch protection cannot
cover tags, so this is a separate ruleset (not part of branch-protections.json).
Apply (the jq filter drops this `_comment`, which the rulesets API rejects):
gh api -X POST repos/cinatra-ai/cinatra/rulesets \
--input <(jq 'del(._comment)' .github/tag-protections.json)
Bypass is the repository Admin role (actor_id 5 — empirically the only base
role id confirmed against this org; org owners hold admin on every org repo,
so the owner who cuts v0.1.0 is covered). Non-numeric base-role ids (write /
maintain) are intentionally NOT used here: their ids are easy to get wrong and
a wrong id could let a plain write collaborator cut a prod-deploying tag.
To let non-admin MAINTAINERS cut releases, create an `@cinatra-ai/maintainers`
team and append it (Team ids are unambiguous):
{ "actor_id": <id from: gh api orgs/cinatra-ai/teams/maintainers --jq .id>,
"actor_type": "Team", "bypass_mode": "always" }
Rules: `creation` lets only bypass actors create `v*` tags; `deletion` +
`non_fast_forward` block deletion and force-moving a release tag for everyone
EXCEPT the admin bypass (admins can still correct a botched tag). For strict
immutability that binds admins too, split into a second no-bypass ruleset.
name: Release tag protection (v*)
